question.text: An open-ended gilt fund must invest at least what percentage of total assets in government securities?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Open-ended gilt funds must invest at least 80% in government securities across maturities. Gilt funds with 10-year constant duration must maintain Macaulay duration equal to 10 years. G-Secs have no default risk but are sensitive to interest rate changes. **The Trap:** 95% is the requirement for ETFs (tracking index securities) and FoFs (in underlying funds), not gilt funds. **Cross-Exam Context:** Gilt fund specifications are tested in V-A and X-A. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Gilt funds are the purest interest rate play — recommend during expected rate-cutting cycles for capital gains. In rising rate environments, gilt funds can suffer significant MTM losses.
